text:
Bayesian Networks (BNs) are a popular class of probabilistic models for reasoning under uncertainty. Their application reaches from medicine and epidemiology over economics and social sciences. Bayesian Networks are particularly useful for evaluating outcomes of interventions in complex systems, and answering multi-conditionals (or what-if) questions. It is however challenging to design an optimal structure for an Abstract Bayesian Network that accurately and precisely captures causal relations among variables of interest. Current methods for designing a structure for an Abstract Bayesian Network include: 1) model search on data, 2) based on domain expertise knowledge, and 3) by means of hybrid approaches.

Learning Bayesian Networks - A Combinatorial Perspective Bayesian Networks (BNs) represent complex real-world phenomena by a directed acyclic graph (DAG). Once the structure and parameters are given, BNs can be employed as probabilistic models for predicting variables from their observed realizations and, vice versa, for estimating conditional probabilistic distributions from known instances or samples. Thus, structure learning corresponds to the task of identifying the conditional independence relationships among the variables encoded by the graph and this goal motivates the design of combinatorial algorithms for learning BNs efficiently and reliably. Recently, considerable efforts have been directed toward the implementation and analysis of new methods in the learning Bayesian networks (LBN) area. In this work, we survey 74 representative instances, classified into three generations of instances ranging from the first models and state-of-the-art implementations to their present improved variants, designed to provide high precision and accuracy for learning Bayesian Networks. We describe each method under consistent terminology, explaining its origins and major commonalities and differences while providing a thorough overview of BN models and related concepts, analysis of present evaluation criteria and performance validation relative to the literature, approaches designed for dealing with different sources of noice in the data and ways for incorporating prior knowledge from domain experts, to facilitate meaningful learning for a useful BN model.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
